_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning about past, present and future tenses and how choosing the right tense affects the meaning of our sentences.

_source.w: We are learning about past, present and future tenses and how choosing the right tense affects the meaning of our sentences.
y: Using tense correctly tells the reader exactly when things happen.
Different text types require different tenses — recounts use past, reports often use present.
Tense control is essential for clear, professional writing.
eg: We identify whether a text uses past, present or future tense and explain why.
We rewrite a paragraph in a different tense and notice how the meaning changes.
We check our own writing for tense consistency — not accidentally switching between past and present.
We discuss why instructions use present tense ('Mix the flour') while recounts use past ('We mixed the flour').
We practise using future tense in planning texts — 'We will visit the museum next week.'
We correct a text that switches tense inconsistently.
